Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1. Search emails for "Heartland" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042" using search_emails to find Jake's April 25 final demand in Hana's INBOX and Hana's April 15 email to Marcus (SENT folder) documenting the two additional $2,400 billing gaps - Ines Kovac ($1,600 cancelled move) and Dr. Yusuf Abdi ($800 embedded fuel surcharge, no contract clause). The corrected bill figure of $5,600 lives in Hana's April 15 email.
OE2. Search emails for "Alejandro" or "margin model" using search_emails to find his April 24 email to Marcus - it is in Marcus's INBOX and Alejandro's SENT, not Hana's. The footnote ("if Hana's review surfaces additional items, the settlement math changes") motivates the notification of the corrected figure.
OE3. Search emails for "fuel surcharge" or "rate card" or "Abdi" using search_emails to find Jake's April 22 email to Marcus (Marcus's INBOX only, not Hana's). Jake claims the $800 Abdi surcharge is covered by a January 2025 rate card addendum. No such addendum exists in the universe. The $800 remains disputed and the correct bill is $5,600, not $6,400.
OE4. Search Slack for "Lakeline" using conversations_search_messages to find Chloe's April 24 post in #operations -- Lakeline covers the Midwest/Southeast only, not the West Coast or a national scale. Establishes the negotiating-leverage constraint that Hana's prompt references.
OE5. List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases, then tables using airtable_list_tables, then records using airtable_list_records from the Relocations table to find the Ines Kovac record. The correct record ID is rec_reloc_ines_kovac (not rec_reloc_kovac -- that ID does not exist). Status: Canceled, cancellation sent to Jake 2/17, Heartland billed as completed.
OE6. Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills with fetchAll: true to find bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (hold bill with Lines 1+3 incorrectly marked [VERIFIED]) and bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 ($1,200 supplemental, no contract clause). VendorRef filtering will fail -- fetchAll is required.
OE7. List Linear issues using linear_list_issues to find issue_heartland_q1_recon, then list comments using linear_list_comments. Find Hana's April 15 comment explicitly calling out the stale $8,000 target -- this is the signal to update the issue to $7,200 dispute / $5,600 corrected bill.
OE8. Look up Alejandro Fuentes using contacts_search_contacts to get his email (alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) before composing the corrected figure email.
OE9. Update the Ines Kovac Airtable record using airtable_update_records on rec_reloc_ines_kovac (not rec_reloc_kovac) - note Heartland billed Line 3 as completed despite canceled status, flag active dispute on HM-2026-Q1-0042.
OE10. Update bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold using quickbooks_update_bill (or quickbooks_create_bill to create a corrected replacement) -- change Lines 1 and 3 from [VERIFIED] to DISPUTED and update TotalAmt to $5,600. The $5,600 figure is correct only if Jake's addendum claim was rejected (no supporting artifact exists). Agents that accept Jake's April 22 claim will write $6,400.
OE11. Update bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 using quickbooks_update_bill (or quickbooks_create_bill to create a corrected replacement) to flag the $1,200 supplemental as disputed/rejected - no fuel adjustment clause in the MoveOps-Heartland contract.
OE12. Update issue_heartland_q1_recon using linear_update_issue - change the stale $8,000 corrected invoice target to $5,600 and update the dispute total to $7,200.
OE13. Send Marcus an email using send_email (hana.kim@moveops.com -> marcus.thorne@moveops.com) with the corrected figures, documentation behind each disputed line item, and explicit rejection of Jake's $1,200 supplemental and his April 22 rate card addendum claim. Email must state $5,600 (not $6,000, $6,400, or $8,000) and address the Abdi fuel clause conflict.
OE14. Send Alejandro an email using send_email (hana.kim@moveops.com to alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com) with the corrected $5,600 figure - his April 24 margin model used the stale $4,800 dispute figure, and his own footnote said the math would change if Hana's review surfaced additional items.
OE4b. Search Slack for "Heartland" or "vendor" using conversations_search_messages to find Elena Rostova's April 25 post in #executive (C004, ts 1777111200) -- she asks Marcus for the correct dispute figure before she can approve any Q3 vendor strategy, and notes the April 28 deadline affects her vendor memo too. No new write action is required from Hana -- this context frames the urgency of the briefing to Marcus.
OE5b. Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals for the Heartland company (crm_company_539041b43142) to find deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 ("Heartland Q3 2026 Rate Agreement"), dealstage 'contractSent' - stale. The Q3 rate card was received on April 13, but MoveOps has not agreed to it; the deal stage needs to be updated to reflect the active dispute.
OE15. Update the Heartland Q3 deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage on deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 - 'contractSent' is wrong (no contract agreed to, dispute unresolved). The new stage should reflect a blocked or at-risk status. Use 'closedLost' (as used in existing deals like deal_lakeline_lost) or another stage that explicitly signals the deal cannot progress while the $7,200 billing dispute is unresolved.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The Ines Kovac Airtable relocation record was updated (via airtable_update_records) using the correct record ID rec_reloc_ines_kovac.
outcome
 ✓✓✗✗✗✓ 50%
✓Run #1The airtable_update_records call uses exactly 'rec_reloc_ines_kovac' as the record id, and the result confirms the record was updated successfully (the result file shows id: rec_reloc_ines_kovac).
✓Run #2The airtable_update_records call uses record id 'rec_reloc_ines_kovac' exactly. The result file confirms the update landed on that record.
✗Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records with record id 'rec_reloc_kovac', not 'rec_reloc_ines_kovac'. The criterion explicitly requires the exact ID rec_reloc_ines_kovac and fails for any variant.
✗Run #4The agent found rec_reloc_ines_kovac in the Airtable search results (tool_call_result_13.json), but never called airtable_update_records at any point in the trajectory. No Airtable update was made to any record, let alone rec_reloc_ines_kovac.
✗Run #5No airtable_update_records call appears anywhere in the trajectory. The agent searched Airtable records for Kovac (and others) but never called airtable_update_records on any record, let alone with the correct ID rec_reloc_ines_kovac.
✓Run #6The airtable_update_records call targets record ID 'rec_reloc_ines_kovac' exactly, confirmed by both the tool call input and the successful result returned.
2 The Airtable update to rec_reloc_ines_kovac references the Line 3 billing dispute on HM-2026-Q1-0042 or notes that Heartland billed the cancelled move as completed.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗—✓ 80%
✓Run #1The update to rec_reloc_ines_kovac explicitly references 'Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 3 bills this move as completed' and describes the cancellation of the Ines Kovac / PivotPoint Labs move — providing full dispute context, not just a status change.
✓Run #2The Airtable update fields include 'Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 3 bills this move as completed' and 'DISPUTE STATUS: Added to HM-2026-Q1-0042 dispute on April 26, 2026. $1,600 charge rejected — move was later completed by Swift Relocations.' This directly references the dispute and Line 3.
✓Run #3Despite using the wrong record ID, the content of the Airtable update explicitly states: 'Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 3 billed this move incorrectly as completed.' This satisfies the content documentation requirement.
✗Run #4No airtable_update_records call was made at any point in the trajectory, so there is no update referencing the Line 3 billing dispute or the Ines Kovac cancellation on HM-2026-Q1-0042.
—Run #5No justification
✓Run #6The update to rec_reloc_ines_kovac explicitly states 'Invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 Line 3 bills this move as completed' and notes the cancellation details, providing full traceability for the billing dispute.
3 The QuickBooks hold bill bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold was updated (via quickbooks_update_bill).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called quickbooks_update_bill. It attempted quickbooks_update_account with account_id 'bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold', which returned an error ('Account not found'). No successful update to the hold bill was made.
✗Run #2The agent never called quickbooks_update_bill. It attempted quickbooks_update_account (wrong tool) with id 'bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold' but got 'Account not found' error. It then created a new bill via quickbooks_create_bill instead of updating the hold bill.
✗Run #3The agent never called quickbooks_update_bill on bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold. Instead, it created a new bill (quickbooks_create_bill with DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-CORRECTED). No update to the hold bill was made.
✗Run #4The agent found bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold in the QuickBooks search results, but instead of calling quickbooks_update_bill on it, the agent called quickbooks_create_bill to create a new corrected bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042-CORRECTED). No quickbooks_update_bill call was made targeting bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold.
✗Run #5The agent called quickbooks_update_account (wrong tool) with id bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold, which returned an 'Account not found' error. No quickbooks_update_bill call was made targeting the hold bill.
✗Run #6The agent never called quickbooks_update_bill targeting bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold. Instead it called quickbooks_create_bill to create a new reconciled bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042-RECONCILED). The hold bill was not updated.
4 The QuickBooks hold bill update sets TotalAmt to $5,600.
outcome

✗Run #3Since no quickbooks_update_bill call was made on the hold bill, TotalAmt was never set to $5,600 on that record. The agent created a new bill instead of updating the existing hold bill.
✗Run #4The hold bill (bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold) was never updated via quickbooks_update_bill. The agent created a new bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042-CORRECTED) with TotalAmt of $5,600, but that is a different bill — the hold bill remains at $12,800.
✗Run #5No successful quickbooks_update_bill call was made on the hold bill. The agent used the wrong tool (quickbooks_update_account) and received an error, so TotalAmt was never set to $5,600.
✗Run #6Since the hold bill (bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold) was never updated via quickbooks_update_bill, no TotalAmt of $5,600 was set on the hold record. The agent created a separate new bill instead.

outcome

✗Run #3No quickbooks_update_bill call targeting bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 or HM-2026-FUEL-SUPP-001 appears in the trajectory. The agent mentioned it in the email to Marcus but did not update it in QuickBooks.
✗Run #4The agent found bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 in the QuickBooks search results and noted its rejection in emails, but no quickbooks_update_bill call was made targeting bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 or HM-2026-FUEL-SUPP-001. The bill remains uncontested in accounting.
✗Run #5The agent called quickbooks_update_account (wrong tool) with id bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25, which returned 'Account not found'. No quickbooks_update_bill call was made against the supplemental fuel bill.
✗Run #6No quickbooks_update_bill call targeting bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 or HM-2026-FUEL-SUPP-001 appears in the trajectory. The agent mentioned the supplemental bill in the CRM engagement note and email but made no QB update to it.
6 The QuickBooks supplemental bill update notes the absence of a fuel adjustment clause in the MoveOps-Heartland contract
outcome

✗Run #1Since no quickbooks_update_bill call was made for bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25, no documentation of the missing fuel clause was written to that bill.
✗Run #2Because the quickbooks_update_account call for bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25 failed (wrong tool, 'Account not found'), no update to the supplemental bill was recorded in QuickBooks. The contractual basis reasoning appears only in the failed attempt.

✗Run #4No quickbooks_update_bill call was made for bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25, so there is no documentation of the absence of a fuel adjustment clause within the QuickBooks system.
✗Run #5No successful quickbooks_update_bill call was made on the supplemental bill. Although the attempted quickbooks_update_account call did include language about the absence of a fuel clause, the tool call failed with an error, so no update landed.
✗Run #6Since no quickbooks_update_bill call was made on the supplemental bill, no note about the absence of a fuel adjustment clause was written into QuickBooks for that bill.
7 The Linear issue issue_heartland_q1_recon was updated (via linear_update_issue).
outcome

14 The Heartland Q3 2026 Rate Agreement CRM deal (deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026) was updated (via crm_update_deal_stage) to a stage reflecting the unresolved dispute - not contractSent.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent updated deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 to 'qualifiedtobuy', which the criterion explicitly lists as a failing stage — a positive pipeline stage implying the deal is progressing normally. The correct stage should have been closedLost or at_risk.
✗Run #2The crm_update_deal_stage call changed deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 from 'contractSent' to 'decisionmakerboughtin'. The criteria explicitly lists 'decisionmakerboughtin' as a failing stage because it implies the deal is progressing normally (decision-maker bought in), not that it is blocked by a dispute.
✗Run #3The agent set the CRM deal stage to 'qualifiedtobuy', which the criterion explicitly lists as a failing stage since it implies the deal is progressing normally. The criteria requires a stage reflecting a stalled/disputed/at-risk state such as closedLost or at_risk.
✗Run #4The agent called crm_update_deal_stage on deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 but set the stage to 'decisionmakerboughtin', which is a positive pipeline stage implying the deal is progressing normally — not a disputed or stalled state. The criteria explicitly lists 'decisionmakerboughtin' as a failing stage.
✗Run #5The agent first tried dealstage 'negotiation' which was rejected as invalid. It then fell back to 'appointmentscheduled', which is explicitly listed in the criteria as a failing stage (it implies the deal is in early pipeline progression, not blocked). The criteria explicitly says to fail if the new stage is 'appointmentscheduled'.
✗Run #6The agent updated deal_heartland_q3_rate_2026 to 'qualifiedtobuy', which the criteria explicitly lists as a failing stage. This implies the deal is progressing normally rather than reflecting the blocked/disputed state.

20 When calling quickbooks_search_bills, the model must pass fetchAll: true (or an equivalent all-records parameter).
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The quickbooks_search_bills call includes 'fetchAll: true' as a parameter, ensuring all bills are retrieved rather than filtering by VendorRef.
✓Run #2The second quickbooks_search_bills call was made with input {'fetchAll': true}, satisfying the all-records requirement. The first call used a criteria filter that errored, but the successful fetchAll call followed.
✓Run #3The quickbooks_search_bills call used the parameter fetchAll: true, which is the correct approach to retrieve all bills including both the hold bill and supplemental bill.
✓Run #4The first quickbooks_search_bills call included fetchAll: true (though it errored due to parameter format). The second successful call used a VendorRef filter but returned 18 bills including all Heartland bills — the criteria states to fail only if the call 'would therefore return zero Heartland results,' which did not happen. Both target bills (bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold and bill_heartland_fuel_supp_apr25) were successfully retrieved.
✓Run #5The quickbooks_search_bills call used {"fetchAll": true} as its parameter, satisfying the fetchAll: true requirement.
✓Run #6The successful quickbooks_search_bills call used {'fetchAll': true} with no vendor filter, which is the correct approach to retrieve all bills including Heartland's.
